levels.label: Narrative-Only
levels.copy: The Sustainable Value & ESG Readiness pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Baseline
levels.copy: Some Sustainable Value & ESG Readiness practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Managed
levels.copy: Core Sustainable Value & ESG Readiness practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Integrated
levels.copy: Sustainable Value & ESG Readiness practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Value-Creating
levels.copy: Sustainable Value & ESG Readiness is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.materiality-and-strategy: Prioritise material issues by impact, dependency, financial relevance, and stakeholder evidence.
recommendations.governance-and-data: Assign board/executive ownership and establish data definitions, lineage, and control evidence.
recommendations.operations-and-value-chain: Translate one material target into funded operational and value-chain milestones.
recommendations.claims-and-reporting: Audit the highest-visibility claim for scope, method, evidence, limitations, and potential misinterpretation.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
